title: What does a fine-tune actually learn?
subtitle: A fine-tune is a routing change, not a concept-addition. We trained a personal-style LoRA on 20 GitHub repos and looked inside.
summary: We trained a personal-style LoRA on Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B and read it three ways: SAE drift, full Goodfire VPD with adversarial PGD, and multi-layer integrated-gradients attribution. The headline SAE result almost shipped — but the same labels appeared on a BASE-model control. What actually changed is routing: alive component counts triple, attention reweights, and pub use crate:: predicts 'config' (the author's actual crate name) instead of 'error'.
